title: The Big Parade

evidence.note: Published 1925; the 95-year US copyright term has expired for works published through 1930, so the film print is public domain in the US by term expiry.

watch.label: Internet Archive — The Big Parade (1925), full film

editorial: The Big Parade is a 1925 American silent war drama directed by King Vidor for Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer, starring John Gilbert and Renee Adoree from a story by World War I veteran Laurence Stallings. A landmark anti-war film and one of the highest-grossing pictures of the silent era, its success helped establish MGM as a major studio; it entered the National Film Registry in 1992. Published in 1925, the film print's 95-year US copyright term has expired, placing the photoplay in the public domain by term expiry.
